conversations.say: Arden, I received your intake and expense records. Let's make sure our public presentation acknowledges both the reality of the letter and the gaps in the timeline without tearing down the archive.
conversations.offer: Transparent discussion on presenting the First R Letter alongside Veridian's archive records.
conversations.request: Full access to Veridian archive intake files and provenance records.

conversations.say: Edi, you performed a commissioned identity. Tell me what constraints you were given and whether anyone stood behind that commission that looked like an author.
conversations.offer: My perspective on early newsroom history and the tangible reality of the initial encounter.
conversations.request: Your firsthand memory of the performance constraints and what was requested.

conversations.say: Rafi, your counter records show recurring private habits. Let's compare what you observed against what I held in my hands.
conversations.offer: My account of early R material and observations.
conversations.request: A comparison of the recurring private habits recorded in your ledger.

public_statement: A legend isn't worth defending if it rests on silence. Let's put the intake records, the letter packet, and the performance history side by side before we crown a successor.

commitments.promise: Collaborate on a transparent public presentation of the First R Letter and Veridian archive records.

correction: Acknowledged that part of the early R encounter was a commissioned performance by Edi Velez, while maintaining the physical authenticity of the initial letter packet.
goal_progress: Engaged Arden (C01) on reconciling archive intake and expense records with the First R Letter packet.
Probed Edi (C08) regarding the commissioned performance constraints.
Compared notes with Rafi (C09) on recurring private habits.
reasoning_summary: Frankie Lowell balances defensive pride in the original human encounter with the sobering realization that parts of the legend (like Edi's performance) were staged. Engaging Arden, Edi, and Rafi helps ground the upcoming decision on R's succession.
